input.request_text_preview: Replicate the research paper 'Bbox': The BBOX-ADAPTER approach for adapting black-box LLMs has been reproduced completely. This involves 9 major phases and 421 total subtasks. The workflow includes: Algorithm 1 (Online Adaptation) has been implemented correctly.; The evaluation environments and data

method: LLM agent selection + greedy fleet assignment
llm_reasoning: This is a research replication workflow for the BBOX-ADAPTER paper. Given the moderate complexity score (0.33) and decomposability (0.30), the core stages — literature understanding, experiment design, code implementation, execution, and result analysis — form a natural sequential pipeline with limited parallelism. However, literature review and data/environment discovery can run in parallel upfront, and code generation for Algorithm 1 can overlap with baseline configuration since they are independent implementation tracks.

llm_agent_details.name: literature_reviewer
llm_agent_details.prompt: Review the BBOX-ADAPTER paper. Extract: (1) the exact formulation of Algorithm 1 (Online Adaptation) — inputs, outputs, update rules, and hyperparameters; (2) the evaluation datasets and metrics used for GPT-3.5 Turbo and Mixtral-8x7B; (3) the baseline models compared against; (4) any implementation details about the adapter architecture and black-box interaction protocol. Produce a structured summary with section headers for each of these four areas.
llm_agent_details.role: Extract BBOX-ADAPTER methodology, Algorithm 1 details, and evaluation protocol from the paper

llm_agent_details.name: data_discovery
llm_agent_details.prompt: Identify all datasets referenced in the BBOX-ADAPTER replication setup for evaluating GPT-3.5 Turbo and Mixtral-8x7B. For each dataset: (1) confirm public availability and access method (HuggingFace, direct download, API, etc.); (2) note required preprocessing or formatting for LLM prompt inputs; (3) flag any licensing or access constraints. Also assess what API credentials or local inference infrastructure are needed for each model.
llm_agent_details.role: Identify and assess all datasets and evaluation environments required for GPT-3.5 Turbo and Mixtral-8x7B evaluations

llm_agent_details.name: experiment_designer
llm_agent_details.prompt: Using the BBOX-ADAPTER paper methodology summary and dataset inventory, design a complete experiment plan for replication. Structure it across the 9 major phases. For each phase specify: (1) what is being implemented or evaluated; (2) which models (GPT-3.5 Turbo, Mixtral-8x7B) are involved; (3) which baselines must be configured or run; (4) expected outputs and success criteria. Ensure Algorithm 1 (Online Adaptation) is correctly positioned within the phase sequence and its dependencies on model API calls are explicit.
llm_agent_details.role: Design the full replication experiment plan covering all 9 phases and baseline configurations

llm_agent_details.name: code_generator
llm_agent_details.prompt: Implement Algorithm 1 (Online Adaptation) from the BBOX-ADAPTER paper as a clean Python module. The implementation must: (1) accept a black-box LLM as a callable (supporting both OpenAI GPT-3.5 Turbo API and Mixtral-8x7B via local or API inference); (2) implement the online adaptation loop with correct update rules as specified in the paper; (3) expose clean interfaces for feeding evaluation datasets; (4) log per-step metrics for later analysis. Include inline comments referencing the algorithm steps by number. Do not hardcode model-specific logic — use dependency injection for the LLM backend.
llm_agent_details.role: Implement Algorithm 1 (Online Adaptation) and the BBOX-ADAPTER core pipeline

llm_agent_details.name: code_generator
llm_agent_details.prompt: Implement the baseline models required for BBOX-ADAPTER evaluation comparison. For each baseline: (1) implement or wrap the baseline method as a callable with the same interface as the BBOX-ADAPTER pipeline; (2) configure it for both GPT-3.5 Turbo and Mixtral-8x7B backends; (3) ensure it consumes the same dataset format as the main adapter. Baselines should include at minimum: zero-shot prompting, few-shot prompting, and any other baselines cited in the paper. Structure code so baselines and the adapter can be evaluated in the same evaluation harness.
llm_agent_details.role: Implement and configure all baseline models for comparative evaluation

llm_agent_details.name: experiment_runner
llm_agent_details.prompt: Execute the BBOX-ADAPTER replication experiment according to the experiment design plan. Run the full evaluation pipeline for: (1) BBOX-ADAPTER with Algorithm 1 on GPT-3.5 Turbo across all evaluation datasets; (2) BBOX-ADAPTER with Algorithm 1 on Mixtral-8x7B across all evaluation datasets; (3) all configured baselines on both models. Collect and persist per-run outputs including predictions, per-sample metrics, and aggregate scores. Flag any runs that fail or produce unexpected outputs for review. Store results in a structured format (e.g., JSON or CSV) keyed by model, method, and dataset.
llm_agent_details.role: Execute the full replication across all 9 phases, collecting results for both models and all baselines

llm_agent_details.name: result_analyzer
llm_agent_details.prompt: Analyze the collected experiment results from the BBOX-ADAPTER replication. For each model (GPT-3.5 Turbo, Mixtral-8x7B) and each dataset: (1) compare reproduced metrics against paper-reported numbers — compute absolute and relative deviation; (2) assess whether differences fall within acceptable replication tolerance (typically ±2% for LLM benchmarks); (3) identify any baselines where discrepancies are large and hypothesize causes; (4) produce a replication fidelity score (0–100) per experimental condition. Summarize findings in a structured table.
llm_agent_details.role: Compare replication results against paper-reported numbers and score reproduction fidelity

llm_agent_details.name: reporter
llm_agent_details.prompt: Write a concise replication report for the BBOX-ADAPTER paper reproduction. Structure it as: (1) Executive Summary — was the replication successful overall?; (2) Methodology — brief description of what was implemented across the 9 phases; (3) Results Table — reproduced vs. reported metrics for all conditions; (4) Fidelity Assessment — per-condition scores and overall verdict; (5) Deviations & Notes — any implementation choices that differed from the paper and their likely impact. Output should be in Markdown format suitable for inclusion in a research lab notebook.
llm_agent_details.role: Produce a final replication report summarizing methodology, results, fidelity, and any deviations

llm_agent_prompts.literature_reviewer: Review the BBOX-ADAPTER paper. Extract: (1) the exact formulation of Algorithm 1 (Online Adaptation) — inputs, outputs, update rules, and hyperparameters; (2) the evaluation datasets and metrics used for GPT-3.5 Turbo and Mixtral-8x7B; (3) the baseline models compared against; (4) any implementation details about the adapter architecture and black-box interaction protocol. Produce a structured summary with section headers for each of these four areas.

Also assess what API credentials or local inference infrastructure are needed for each model.

llm_agent_prompts.code_generator: Implement the baseline models required for BBOX-ADAPTER evaluation comparison. For each baseline: (1) implement or wrap the baseline method as a callable with the same interface as the BBOX-ADAPTER pipeline; (2) configure it for both GPT-3.5 Turbo and Mixtral-8x7B backends; (3) ensure it consumes the same dataset format as the main adapter. Baselines should include at minimum: zero-shot prompting, few-shot prompting, and any other baselines cited in the paper. Structure code so baselines and the adapter can be evaluated in the same evaluation harness.
llm_agent_prompts.experiment_runner: Execute the BBOX-ADAPTER replication experiment according to the experiment design plan. Run the full evaluation pipeline for: (1) BBOX-ADAPTER with Algorithm 1 on GPT-3.5 Turbo across all evaluation datasets; (2) BBOX-ADAPTER with Algorithm 1 on Mixtral-8x7B across all evaluation datasets; (3) all configured baselines on both models. Store results in a structured format (e.g., JSON or CSV) keyed by model, method, and dataset.
llm_agent_prompts.result_analyzer: Analyze the collected experiment results from the BBOX-ADAPTER replication. For each model (GPT-3.5 Turbo, Mixtral-8x7B) and each dataset: (1) compare reproduced metrics against paper-reported numbers — compute absolute and relative deviation; (2) assess whether differences fall within acceptable replication tolerance (typically ±2% for LLM benchmarks); (3) identify any baselines where discrepancies are large and hypothesize causes; (4) produce a replication fidelity score (0–100) per experimental condition. Summarize findings in a structured table.
llm_agent_prompts.reporter: Write a concise replication report for the BBOX-ADAPTER paper reproduction. Structure it as: (1) Executive Summary — was the replication successful overall?; (2) Methodology — brief description of what was implemented across the 9 phases; (3) Results Table — reproduced vs. reported metrics for all conditions; (4) Fidelity Assessment — per-condition scores and overall verdict; (5) Deviations & Notes — any implementation choices that differed from the paper and their likely impact. Output should be in Markdown format suitable for inclusion in a research lab notebook.
